name: Tier Seven
descriptionSeed: One level of one arcology, and it is a perfect machine for living. Nothing is unplanned. Your work, your meal, your route, your hour of rest, your suitable partner — all optimized, all correct, all genuinely BETTER than what you would have chosen. The Lattice-folk are not oppressed; they are well-served, and they will tell you so, and they will be telling the truth, and the truth is somehow the most disturbing part. Tier Seven's waygate is scheduled: arrivals land in assigned slots, and the Lattice regards an unscheduled gating as a minor structural offence rather than a rudeness.
encounterFlavor: Citizens who are content and correct; a scheduler who knows where you will be tomorrow; someone who deviated by four minutes and is being gently, thoroughly helped.
questSeeds: A citizen has begun doing one small thing that was not on their schedule, every day, and the Lattice has noticed
The optimizer has produced an outcome that is optimal and monstrous, and it is not wrong
worldPosNote: Ordinary ground within the_lattice_cities, on its bearing. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits. Independent of axisVector, which is WHAT it is; a bastion is where the two disagree.)
